sourceMovie.title: A Private Life
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: A Private Life (2025) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Rebecca Zlotowski's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Client
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Private Life clicked for you, The Client (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Private Life?
faq.answer: Try The Client, The Night of the 12th, Solace—each shares DNA with A Private Life while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Private Life?
faq.answer: A Private Life (2025) is best described as Drama, Mystery, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Private Life worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
